_note: IG carousel config for chicken-bone-broth. Liquid topper recipe — imagery is more about the process and the jar-to-bowl transfer than plating.

slides.prompt: Cinematic food photography, eye-level slight-angle shot of a tall clear mason jar filled with golden-amber chicken bone broth, visibly gelatinous with small bits of collagen swirling at the surface, gently steaming. The jar sits on a weathered oak counter. Deep warm side-light from the right casting a long dramatic shadow of the jar to the left. A small rustic ladle rests beside the jar. Rustic kitchen background softly out of focus (stoneware, wooden shelves). Shot on 50mm at f/2.8, warm tungsten highlights, shallow depth of field. Home kitchen — not studio. Honest and golden, the steam is the hero. No text, no labels, no watermarks, no logos, no people, no dogs, no brand marks on the jar.

Cinematic overhead flat-lay food photography with dramatic side-light from the upper right casting long warm shadows across a weathered oak surface: a pile of raw chicken bones (backs and necks) on parchment paper, a glass jar of clear cool water, a small unmarked glass bottle of apple cider vinegar, a single whole orange carrot (unpeeled) and two celery stalks lying side by side. Generous negative space between items. Warm tungsten-toned light, deep shadows. Absolutely no onion, no garlic, no salt, no seasoning. No text on anything, no writing, no watermarks, no logos, no jars with labels, no utensils, no hands, no people, no dogs.

Cinematic close-up food photography at an eye-level 25-degree angle: a large cast-iron stockpot on a stovetop, lid partially ajar, golden-amber broth visibly simmering inside with chicken bones and chopped vegetables (carrot, celery) breaking the surface. Steam rising dramatically, catching warm side-light from the right. Deep shadows to the left. Shallow depth of field — the pot rim sharp, the stovetop background softly blurred. Honest home-kitchen feel, no studio gloss. No text, no labels, no logos, no hands, no people, no dogs.

Cinematic overhead food photography at a slight angle of a stream of finished golden-amber bone broth being poured from a glass measuring cup into a cream-colored ceramic dog bowl containing dry kibble. The broth catches warm side-light from the right, the pour stream sharp and glistening. Visible collagen swirls in the broth. The bowl rests on a weathered oak counter, long dramatic shadow stretching left. Tight crop — bowl and pour stream fill about 70% of frame. Honest, comforting — this is a real meal-topper moment. No text, no labels, no watermarks, no logos, no hands, no people, no dogs.
